name: Parque Natural de la Serra d'Irta — Running circular from Alcalà de Xivert
name_original: Parque Natural de la Serra d'Irta — Running circular desde Alcalà de Xivert
region_intro: Jogging routes around Alcalà De Xivert traverse a diverse landscape, characterized by the rugged Sierra de Irta Natural Park and over 10 kilometers of Mediterranean coastline. The region features a striking contrast between plains and mountains, with some hills reaching 145 meters, offering varied elevations. This area provides a range of terrains suitable for different fitness levels, from gentle coastal paths to more challenging mountain trails. Best jogging routes around Alcalà De Xivert The most popular jogging route is Parque Natural de la Serra d'Irta — Running circular from Alcalà de Xivert, an 11.1 miles (17.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete. This difficult route leads through rugged landscapes and pine forests within the natural park, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local runners is Cala Mundina – Irta Lighthouse loop from Alcossebre, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.1 km) path. This route follows the scenic coastline, passing coves and offering views of the Irta Lighthouse. Local runners also love the Alcossebre Coastal Path – Cala Mundina loop from Platja Manyetes, an 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail leading through coastal areas and beaches, often completed in about 1 hour 23 minutes. Jogging around Alcalà De Xivert is defined by coastal paths, mountain trails, and natural park routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal runs to more demanding climbs. The routes in Alcalà De Xivert are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 2,600 runners have used komoot to explore Alcalà De Xivert's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: Alcalà De Xivert offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 100 trails available. These include 16 easy routes, 55 moderate routes, and 34 difficult routes, catering to all fitness levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Alcalà De Xivert?
A: The region boasts diverse terrain, from gentle coastal paths along its 10 kilometers of Mediterranean coastline to more challenging mountain trails within the rugged Sierra de Irta Natural Park. You'll find routes through pine forests, along ancient paths, and even some with varied elevations as mountains descend directly to the sea.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Alcalà De Xivert has 16 easy running routes perfect for beginners or families. Many of these are found along the scenic coastline, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40374598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Alcossebre – Playa Romana loop from Platja Manyetes&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a gentle 4.3-mile (7 km) path with minimal elevation, ideal for a relaxed jog with sea views.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while jogging in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: Many routes offer stunning views and pass by significant landmarks. You can run towards the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3409370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boixar Ravine&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic vistas from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/22046351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Mundina – Irta Lighthouse loop from Alcossebre&lt;/a&gt;, which provides views of the Irta Lighthouse and the Mediterranean. The Xivert Castle, perched atop a hill, also offers rewarding views after a more demanding climb.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, the region offers several long-distance and difficult routes. A prime example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e881211574/parque-natural-de-la-serra-d-irta-running-circular-from-alcala-de-xivert" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Serra d'Irta — Running circular from Alcalà de Xivert&lt;/a&gt;, an 11.1-mile (17.9 km) trail with significant elevation gain, leading through rugged landscapes and pine forests.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Alcalà De Xivert are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/22046007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcossebre Coastal Path – Cala Mundina loop from Platja Manyetes&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.3-mile (13.4 km) circular trail that takes you through coastal areas and beaches.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: Alcalà De Xivert is suitable for outdoor activities year-round due to its Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for running, while summer mornings and evenings are ideal for coastal runs to enjoy the sea breeze. Winter provides cooler conditions, perfect for more strenuous mountain trails.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by natural features like lakes or gorges?
A: Yes, the area around Alcalà De Xivert features several natural attractions. While jogging, you might encounter highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1657526" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prat de Cabanes-Torreblanca Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which includes lagoons such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5654440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullal de l'Estany&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique wetland scenery.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: The running trails in Alcalà De Xivert are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. Runners often praise the stunning coastal views, the variety of terrain from beaches to mountains, and the well-maintained paths that make exploring the diverse landscapes a truly enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of the sea?
A: Absolutely. Alcalà De Xivert's extensive coastline means many routes offer spectacular sea views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40373491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manyetes Beach – Capicorb Castle loop from Alcossebre&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, providing a 6.8-mile (11 km) coastal run with refreshing sea breezes and picturesque scenery.

Q: Is public transport available to access the running trails?
A: While specific public transport details for each trailhead are not provided, Alcalà De Xivert and its coastal towns like Alcossebre have local bus services that can help you reach various starting points for trails. It's advisable to check local schedules for the most up-to-date information on routes and timings.

Q: Where can I park my car when accessing the running routes?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the main towns and coastal areas of Alcalà De Xivert and Alcossebre. Many trailheads, especially those near beaches or popular attractions, will have designated parking areas. For routes within the Sierra de Irta Natural Park, look for parking facilities at common access points.
tips: The top of Les Campanilles at 520 meters above sea level is one of the highest points within the Parc Natural de la Serra d'Irta. From here you will have amazing panoramic views in all directions. It can be reached by several trails of varying technical difficulty depending on the choice of each one.
